data.title: Road King Wins Broad Creditor Backing and Extends Restructuring Deadline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Road King Infrastructure ( (HK:1098) ) has issued an update.

Road King Infrastructure has secured substantial creditor backing for its ongoing debt restructuring, with holders representing close to 70% of the principal of its existing notes and perpetual securities having signed or submitted accessions to its restructuring support agreement. The company views this level of support as validation of the terms on offer and a signal of positive momentum as it advances towards implementing the restructuring plan.

To facilitate additional creditors joining the process, Road King has extended the deadline for qualifying for its general RSA fee to 5:00 p.m. Hong Kong time on 7 August 2026. The company is now working with consenting creditors on scheme-related documentation and establishing a liquidating special purpose vehicle to hold creditor interests, while warning investors to consider the risks and awaiting further updates as the restructuring progresses.

More about Road King Infrastructure

Road King Infrastructure Limited is a Hong Kong-listed infrastructure and property company incorporated in Bermuda and traded under stock code 1098. The group focuses on infrastructure-related investments and has issued various notes and perpetual securities to finance its operations and growth, making it an active participant in regional debt capital markets.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Lufax Holding Ltd Class A ( (HK:6623) ) has shared an update.

Lufax Holding Ltd reported that it continues to work toward resuming trading in Hong Kong, updating investors on investigations into historical transactions, internal control reviews and progress in addressing questions from the Stock Exchange. The company has brought its SEC filings up to date, regained compliance with NYSE listing requirements and maintained normal business operations during the suspension period.

The board detailed operational highlights for the second quarter of 2026 and emphasized that the group’s business activities have proceeded uninterrupted despite the trading halt. Lufax also announced significant governance changes, including the resignation of several non-executive directors and its chief financial officer, the appointment of a new independent non-executive director and authorized representative, and a reconfiguration of board committees to strengthen oversight and align with resumption requirements.

More about Lufax Holding Ltd Class A

The group provides technology-driven lending and wealth management services, operating through a network of subsidiaries and consolidated entities to serve individual and SME borrowers.

data.title: Wang On Group and China Agri-Products Exchange Seal RMB64 Million Sale-Leaseback with Haier
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions

Wang On Group Limited ( (HK:1222) ) has shared an update.

Wang On Group and China Agri-Products Exchange have entered into a sale and leaseback arrangement through their subsidiaries Qinzhou Hongjin and Kaifeng Hongjin, selling certain leased assets to Haier for RMB64 million, equivalent to about HK$73.81 million. The transaction, agreed after arm’s length negotiations and supported by equity pledge registrations from related entities, provides liquidity while allowing continued use of the assets, and qualifies as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong Listing Rules for both companies.

Under the terms, Haier will pay the sale price within 10 business days after receiving required receipts, payment notices, and confirmation of equity pledge registrations, reflecting a loan-to-value structure acceptable to the lessor. This financing move strengthens the groups’ capital flexibility without immediate operational disruption, potentially improving balance sheet management and signaling a strategic use of asset-backed funding to support ongoing projects and stakeholder confidence.

More about Wang On Group Limited

Wang On Group Limited and China Agri-Products Exchange Limited are Hong Kong-listed companies involved in agricultural products exchange and related property and infrastructure operations in mainland China. Their activities include developing and managing agri-product marketplaces and associated facilities, with a focus on leveraging financial arrangements to support asset-based growth and operations.

data.title: Shaanxi Micot Completes Phase II Data Lock for Stroke Drug MT200605
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Shaanxi Micot Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2335) ) has issued an update.

Shaanxi Micot Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. has completed the database lock for a Phase II clinical trial of its independently developed neuroprotective drug candidate MT200605 for acute ischemic stroke in China. MT200605 is an injectable agent that combines TrkB receptor activation with oxygen free radical scavenging to limit neuronal damage and promote regeneration in stroke patients.

The Phase II trial was a multi-center, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study evaluating the efficacy, safety and pharmacokinetics of MT200605 in 360 patients, with first enrollment in September 2025 and last patient out in June 2026. Completion of the database lock marks a key milestone in the program’s clinical development, paving the way for data analysis that could shape the company’s stroke portfolio and influence its competitive position in neurovascular therapeutics.

More about Shaanxi Micot Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shaanxi Micot Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based pharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative injectable neuroprotective therapies. The group targets serious neurological conditions such as acute ischemic stroke, leveraging proprietary drug candidates designed to modulate neuronal survival, reduce oxidative damage and enhance neural repair in hospital and specialist care settings.

Average Trading Volume: 1,224,409

Current Market Cap: HK$9.63B

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited ( (HK:0149) ) has issued an announcement.

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited and its parent Wang On Group Limited have entered into a sale and leaseback arrangement through their subsidiaries Qinzhou Hongjin and Kaifeng Hongjin with Haier as lessor, involving assets sold for RMB64 million, or about HK$73.81 million. The price was set after arm’s-length negotiations, referencing asset costs and an acceptable loan-to-value ratio, and will be paid within 10 business days once specified conditions, including completion of equity pledge registrations by Henan Gangan and Crown Fortress in favour of Haier, are satisfied.

The transaction qualifies as a discloseable transaction for both companies under Hong Kong Listing Rules, as the highest applicable percentage ratio exceeds 5% but is below 25%. This financing move underscores the group’s use of asset-backed structures to unlock liquidity while retaining operational use of the assets, which may bolster its capital flexibility and support ongoing operations without triggering more stringent shareholder approval requirements.

More about China Agri-Products Exchange Limited

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates within the agricultural products and related exchange sector, focusing on the development and operation of agri-product trading and logistics platforms in mainland China. It is affiliated with Wang On Group Limited, which has diversified interests and uses project companies such as Qinzhou Hongjin and Kaifeng Hongjin to hold and manage specific agricultural market assets.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Lens Technology Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6613) ) just unveiled an update.

Lens Technology’s wholly owned subsidiary Lens International (HK) Limited has signed a memorandum of understanding with Intel Corporation to explore cooperation in through-glass via advanced packaging technology. The discussions will cover processes such as glass substrate via formation, high-precision laser processing and metal-filled via deposition, with any concrete engineering validation or volume production to be governed by separate agreements.

Beyond TGV, the parties plan to evaluate potential collaboration in structural components and modules for AI PCs, high-reliability thermal solutions for data center servers and hardware for embodied AI robotics and edge computing. While most provisions of the memorandum are non-binding and effective for one year, Lens Technology’s board expects the framework to deepen its strategic ties with a major semiconductor player, potentially enhancing its role in advanced packaging and AI-related hardware supply chains once specific agreements are reached.

Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited announced a significant change in its shareholder structure after Prosperity Cleanness Investment Holdings Limited sold 175,476,150 shares, equal to about 9% of the company’s issued share capital. data.title: China Power flags up to 57% profit drop on weaker wind and solar, softer coal demand
data.description:

  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) has issued an announcement.

China Power International Development warned that profit attributable to ordinary shareholders for the first half of 2026 is expected to fall to about RMB1.1 billion to RMB1.4 billion, a drop of roughly 45% to 57% from a year earlier. The company cited weaker wind and solar resources, heightened tariff volatility in China’s competitive power market and lower coal-fired electricity sales as key drivers of the earnings decline.

Management said strong rainfall boosted hydropower output at subsidiary SPIC Hydropower, delivering a significant profit increase that partially offset the shortfall in other segments. The figures are based on unaudited internal accounts, and the company plans to release its formal interim results in late August 2026, while advising shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its shares.

Beijing Shougang LanzaTech Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2553) ) has shared an announcement.

Beijing Shougang LanzaTech Technology Co., Ltd. has formalized a governance change under its Articles of Association, eliminating its Supervisory Committee and transferring its oversight functions and powers to the Audit Committee. The move follows shareholder approval in 2024 and came into effect after the company completed registration of the change with the State Administration for Market Regulation on July 24, 2026.

As a result of this structural shift, former supervisors Mr. Zhao Yucheng, Ms. Xing Fei and Ms. Zhang Ling have ceased to hold their supervisory roles and have indicated they have no disagreements with the company or its board. The board expressed gratitude for their service, signaling an orderly transition in oversight that aligns the company’s governance framework more closely with Hong Kong listing practices and may streamline board-level risk and audit supervision for stakeholders.

Yidu Tech, Inc. ( (HK:2158) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Yidu Tech Inc. has convened its annual general meeting for August 28, 2026 in Beijing, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended March 31, 2026 and vote on a proposed final dividend. The agenda also includes re-election of two independent non-executive directors, renewal of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and broad mandates for the board to issue, allot and transfer shares within defined limits, which would give management continued flexibility in capital management and potential future fundraising.

The share-issuance mandate caps new and treasury share issuances at up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, with an additional buffer linked to shares repurchased under a separate buyback authorization. These resolutions, if passed, will maintain Yidu Tech’s ability to adjust its capital structure in response to market conditions while formalizing board authority over director remuneration and auditor fees, matters of direct interest to shareholders and corporate governance observers.

More about Yidu Tech, Inc.

data.description:

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group ( (HK:1093) ) has shared an update.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group has received marketing approval in mainland China for its independently developed Paliperidone Palmitate Injection, a second-generation long-acting antipsychotic indicated for both acute and maintenance treatment of schizophrenia. Available in multiple strengths, the once-monthly injection is designed to deliver rapid onset without supplemental oral medication, improving adherence and reducing relapse and rehospitalisation rates for patients with poor treatment compliance.

The product has been recommended in leading domestic and international clinical guidelines, including the 2025 Chinese Guidelines for the Prevention and Treatment of Schizophrenia, underscoring its clinical and pharmacoeconomic value. This approval expands CSPC’s psychiatric and neurological portfolio and strengthens its strategic position in the central nervous system drug market, as the company signals ongoing investment in high-value therapies to broaden treatment options for patients.

data.title: China Power’s June Hydropower Surge Fails to Offset First-Half Sales Dip
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) has provided an announcement.

China Power International Development reported that group electricity sales in June 2026 rose 1.45% year on year to 11.46 million MWh, driven largely by strong hydropower output, while total sales for the first half slipped 0.81% to 62.03 million MWh. The shift reflects robust growth in hydropower generation, particularly in Hunan and Guizhou, contrasted with declines in wind, coal-fired and some photovoltaic output, signaling an ongoing transition in the company’s generation mix.

Sales from wholly owned and controlled hydropower plants surged 52% in the first half, offsetting weaker contributions from coal-fired power, which fell nearly 11%, and wind power, which dropped about 14%. Major associates and joint ventures also recorded lower electricity sales, indicating broader softness in traditional and some renewable segments, a development that may weigh on overall utilization rates and underscores the company’s increasing reliance on hydropower assets for growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold
with a HK$3.30 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock,

data.title: Emperor Culture Group Relocates Bermuda Registered Office
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest update is out from Emperor Culture Group ( (HK:0491) ).

Emperor Culture Group Limited has announced that, effective 3 August 2026, its registered office, principal share registrar, and transfer office in Bermuda will relocate to a new address at Richmond House on Par-la-Ville Road in Hamilton. The company emphasized that its Hong Kong branch share registrar and transfer office will remain unchanged, indicating that day-to-day share registration services for Hong Kong investors are unaffected by the move.

The change in Bermuda office location reflects an administrative adjustment rather than a strategic shift in business operations, with no immediate impact signaled for shareholders or stakeholders. The board, led by chairman Yeung Ching Loong, Alexander, continues to oversee corporate governance as the company maintains its existing listing and share registration arrangements in Hong Kong.

More about Emperor Culture Group

Emperor Culture Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating within the broader cultural and entertainment sector. The group’s activities typically span media and culture-related businesses, serving audiences and investors in Hong Kong and overseas markets.

data.title: CWT International Launches Group AI & IT Office to Drive AI-Led Transformation
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

CWT International Limited ( (HK:0521) ) has provided an update.

CWT International has completed an organisational restructuring and set up a Group AI & IT Office in June 2026, consolidating its IT functions and AI expertise under a unified technology framework operated via its wholly owned subsidiary Prism AI Studio Pte. Ltd. The new office will drive group-wide AI initiatives, standardise development, and build reusable AI solutions aimed at reducing transformation costs and improving returns on technology investment.

The company has also signed a memorandum of understanding between Prism AI and its freight logistics arm CWT Globelink to develop an AI-driven pricing solution as a flagship pilot project, targeting the automation of manual, high-volume data tasks. Management expects this systematic AI-driven transformation to create differentiated, technology-enabled advantages, reallocate staff to higher-value roles, and potentially enable future commercialisation of its AI tools, enhancing long-term profitability and shareholder value.

More about CWT International Limited

CWT International Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated company with diversified global operations, including freight logistics through its CWT Globelink business. The group focuses on leveraging technology and digitalization to enhance operational efficiency, support logistics services, and strengthen its competitive position in global supply chain markets.
